payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Policy framework must encompass regulatory compliance requirements
Policy scope must include social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Quality management process execution
payload.justification: Quality management is described as a process framework requiring governance and evidence-based decision making.

payload.description: Objective-setting for quality outcomes
payload.justification: Objective-setting is identified as a key function of the quality policy framework.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance commitment requirement
payload.justification: Commitment to regulatory compliance is defined as a governing rule within the quality policy framework.

payload.description: Continuous improvement mandate
payload.justification: Continuous improvement is both a policy commitment and an annual organizational mandate, establishing it as a governing rule.

payload.description: Quality policy as foundational governance framework
payload.justification: Quality policy is described as a foundational governance framework and policy framework, representing a strategic entity of importance to the enterprise.

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations
payload.justification: Customer needs and expectations are identified as strategic things the organization must understand and exceed.

payload.description: Work environment enabling organizational objectives
payload.justification: The enabling work environment is a strategic entity that leadership commits to creating.

payload.description: External provider relationships
payload.justification: External provider relationships are recognized as strategically important things for value creation.

payload.description: Annual quality improvement cycle
payload.justification: Continual improvement is established as a core annual organizational objective, indicating a yearly strategic cycle.

payload.description: Customers whose needs drive organizational strategy
payload.justification: Customers are identified as key stakeholders whose needs and expectations the organization must understand and exceed.

payload.description: Leadership providing enabling environment
payload.justification: Leadership is identified in its role of creating an enabling work environment for achieving objectives.

payload.description: Engaged people as strategic enablers
payload.justification: People (employees) are recognized as essential stakeholders whose engagement enables business benefits.

payload.description: External providers contributing to value creation
payload.justification: External providers are identified as strategic stakeholders in mutually beneficial relationships.

payload.description: Organizational director providing governance authority
payload.justification: The director role is explicitly identified as providing formal policy authorization and establishing governance authority.

payload.description: Alignment of quality policy with business purpose and context
payload.justification: Establishing organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose is identified as a foundational strategic goal.

payload.description: Exceeding customer expectations
payload.justification: Understanding and exceeding customer expectations is articulated as a strategic organizational driver and goal.

payload.description: Achievement of organizational objectives
payload.justification: Achieving organizational objectives through an enabling work environment is identified as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Realization of business benefits through people engagement
payload.justification: People engagement is recognized as a strategic enabler for achieving business benefits, making this a key goal.

payload.description: Value creation through external partnerships
payload.justification: Value creation through mutually beneficial external provider relationships is identified as a strategic driver.

payload.description: Process approach for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: Process approach is explicitly declared as an organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making
payload.justification: Evidence-based decision making is explicitly identified as a principle for quality management governance.

payload.description: Social responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Social responsibilities are declared as organizational obligations, representing a strategic constraint.

payload.description: Environmental responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Environmental responsibilities are declared as organizational obligations, representing a strategic constraint.

payload.description: Charitable responsibility obligations
payload.justification: Charitable responsibilities are declared as organizational obligations, representing a strategic constraint.

payload.description: Legislative compliance obligations
payload.justification: Legislative responsibilities are declared as organizational obligations, representing a strategic constraint.

payload.description: Objective-setting process
payload.justification: SG448 identifies objective-setting as a core component of the policy framework, representing a business process.

payload.description: Customer requirements fulfillment process
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as requiring understanding needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations—a complete business process.

payload.description: Continual improvement process
payload.justification: SG448 identifies continual improvement as a policy commitment; SG453 defines it as a central business objective across all QMS aspects.

payload.description: People engagement and capability utilization process
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as a core business concept involving recognition and utilization of human capabilities.

payload.description: Interconnected process management workflow
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as managing activities and resources as interconnected processes for efficiency.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making process
payload.justification: SG454 establishes data analysis and evidence gathering as a process preceding quality management system decisions.

payload.description: Quality decisions must be based on analyzed data
payload.justification: SG454 explicitly establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before decisions.

payload.description: Activities and resources must be managed as interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG452 establishes the process approach as a conceptual model requiring activities and resources to be managed as interconnected processes.

payload.description: Quality Policy document
payload.justification: SG447 establishes quality policy as a foundational conceptual artifact, representing a business entity that encapsulates organizational purpose and context.

payload.description: Customer Requirements specification
payload.justification: SG448 identifies customer requirements as part of the policy framework; SG449 reinforces this as a business concept requiring understanding and fulfillment.

payload.description: Regulatory Compliance record
payload.justification: SG448 identifies regulatory compliance as a policy framework component; SG456 extends this to legislative responsibility requirements.

payload.description: Quality Management System
payload.justification: SG453 and SG454 both reference the quality management system as a central business entity subject to improvement and decision-making processes.

payload.description: External Provider Relationship
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model, establishing external provider relationships as distinct business entities.

payload.description: Quality Policy aligns with organizational purpose and context
payload.justification: SG447 describes the alignment relationship between quality policy and organizational purpose/context as foundational.

payload.description: External providers interdependently create value with organization
payload.justification: SG455 explicitly defines interdependent relationships that mutually create value as a conceptual model.

payload.description: Customer
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer as a business actor with needs, requirements, and expectations that must be understood and met.

payload.description: External Provider
payload.justification: SG455 defines external providers as actors in interdependent relationships that mutually create value.

payload.description: Leadership role
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating enabling environments and facilitating objective achievement.

payload.description: Engaged People role
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets, establishing a distinct role.

payload.description: Achieve continual improvement across all quality management system aspects
payload.justification: SG453 explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective.

payload.description: Meet customer requirements and exceed expectations
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as requiring meeting requirements and exceeding expectations, representing a business goal.

payload.description: Enable objective achievement through people involvement
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership's goal as creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.

payload.description: Create mutual value through external relationships
payload.justification: SG455 identifies mutual value creation as the purpose of interdependent external provider relationships.

payload.description: Customer focus drives business understanding and delivery
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, representing a guiding principle.

payload.description: People capabilities are essential organizational assets
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets, establishing a core principle.

payload.description: Activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as a conceptual model, representing a guiding principle for organizational management.

payload.description: Decisions are based on analyzed data and evidence
payload.justification: SG454 establishes evidence-based decision making as a fundamental business principle governing quality management.

payload.description: Quality policy must align with organizational purpose and context
payload.justification: SG447 establishes alignment with organizational purpose and context as a foundational constraint on quality policy.

payload.description: Policy framework must encompass regulatory compliance requirements including social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities
payload.justification: SG448 identifies regulatory compliance as a mandatory component of the policy framework, representing a constraint. | SG456 extends policy scope to encompass multiple responsibility domains, establishing a constraint on policy boundaries.

payload.description: Process approach principle where activities and resources achieve results through interconnected process series
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies a logical management principle articulating design philosophy for process-oriented results achievement, directly mapping to Why/Principle classification

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making principle requiring QMS decisions to follow data and information analysis
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes a logical principle defining decision-making requirements based on analytical evidence, directly mapping to Why/Principle classification

payload.description: Temporal validity period attribute of policy document
payload.justification: Signal references temporal validity as a data element of the policy document, representing a detailed attribute specification for document lifecycle management.

payload.description: Director signature block data structure
payload.justification: Signature block represents a distinct data entity containing organizational authority information. Confidence matches Signal confidence as this is a direct entity interpretation.

payload.description: Director role with signature authority for policy document approval
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies Director as organizational authority role. At Implementer level, this represents the detailed role specification with signature authority interface requirement.

payload.description: Policy document requires Director signature authorization constraint
payload.justification: The signature requirement implies a validation constraint that policy documents must be authorized by Director role before becoming valid.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
